objective: Implement rate limiting on the login endpoint to prevent brute-force attacks. After 5 failed attempts within 60 seconds, the endpoint should return 429 and lock the account for 15 minutes.
scope: Add rate-limiting middleware to the POST /auth/login endpoint
Return HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header when the limit is exceeded
Store attempt counts in Redis with a 60-second TTL
Add an account lock record with a 15-minute TTL on threshold breach
constraints: Do not touch unrelated auth endpoints
Do not modify the User schema or database migrations
No new npm dependencies unless Redis client is not already available
Preserve all existing login success and failure response shapes
acceptance_criteria: POST /auth/login returns 429 after 5 failed attempts within 60 seconds
A Retry-After header is present on 429 responses
Successful logins reset the attempt counter
Account lock expires automatically after 15 minutes
All existing auth tests continue to pass
New integration tests cover the rate-limiting and lock scenarios
review_focus: edge cases around the 60-second window boundary
behavior when Redis is unavailable (fail open vs fail closed)
correctness of the Retry-After header value
state transitions between normal, rate-limited, and locked states
potential for counter bypass via timing attacks
